title: POST /git-token (control socket)
description: Request/response contract for the control-plane endpoint that the git-credential-generacy CLI wrapper calls over the Unix socket at /run/generacy-control-plane/control.sock. Returns a fresh GitHub installation token, served from in-memory cache when valid or freshly fetched from the cloud on-demand pull endpoint (generacy-cloud#817). Pre-expiry refresh fires when expiresAt - now <= 5 minutes.

Request.title: GitTokenRequest
Request.description: POST body (Content-Type: application/json). `credentialId` defaults to the single configured github-app credential when omitted.

credentialId.description: Credential ID from `.agency/credentials.yaml`. Optional in v1 (single github-app credential is implied).

SuccessResponse.title: GitTokenSuccessResponse
SuccessResponse.description: HTTP 200 application/json. The wrapper translates `token` into the password line of the git credential-helper output.

token.description: Opaque GitHub installation token. NEVER log this value.

expiresAt.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp. Always > now() on success; CLOUD_RESPONSE_INVALID otherwise.

code.description: Stable machine-readable error code. The CLI wrapper maps these to stderr messages.

details.description: Optional context (e.g., upstream HTTP status). May omit sensitive material; never includes the token.
